response.text:
The scope is showing an eye/jitter analysis view for Ref 1.

- Main plot: an eye diagram labeled “Plot 1 - Eye Diagram (Meas 1)”
  - Data rate is about 2.501 Gb/s
  - Eye width shown is about 3341/4995 UIs
  - Midlevel is about 80.357 mV
  - The eye is reasonably open, with visible crossing spread and some vertical noise

- Lower-right plot: waveform view of the underlying signal
  - Dense waveform band centered roughly around 0 V
  - Amplitude spans roughly ±500 mV to ±600 mV
  - Orange measurement markers/reference lines are visible

- Left-side analysis plots:
  - TIE Histogram (Meas 1): strong skewed distribution with a dominant cluster on the positive side
  - TIE Spectrum (Meas 1): several discrete spectral components/spurs
  - Bathtub (Meas 1): BER-style bathtub curve with a horizontal marker around very low BER

- Measurement table/results on the right:
  - Meas 1: Jitter Summary
    - DR: 2.501 Gb/s
    - PL(UI): 40
    - TIE(σ): 8.681 ps
    - TJ@: 54.07 ps
    - RJ: 1.680 ps
    - DJ: 33.75 ps
    - DCD: 2.288 ps
    - EW: 345.9 ps
  - Meas 2: Period
    - mean about 1.066 ns
  - Meas 3: Frequency
    - mean about 1.106 GHz
  - Meas 4:
    - Positive Overshoot
    - mean about 6.380%

So the positive overshoot measurement was added successfully, and it’s currently reading about 6.38%.
